retrieved_docs.title: Acute Liver Failure -- Histopathology
retrieved_docs.content: The histopathologic features of acute hepatitis secondary to viral infections usually show intranuclear viral inclusions and surrounding neutrophils. Classical historical features of autoimmune hepatitis include portal inflammation and interface hepatitis, formally known as piecemeal necrosis, which is characterized by the presence of portal inflammatory cells between the portal and liver parenchyma. [10] Additionally, special stains for HBV, CMV, EBV, adenovirus hepatitis, HSV, and immune-histopathological stains should be considered. Increased hepatic copper concentrations and Mallory-Denk bodies are the classical histopathological liver biopsy findings in patients with Wilson's disease. [35]

retrieved_docs.title: Percutaneous Liver Biopsy -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: Liver biopsy findings can have prognostic significance in the evaluation of liver disease. It can be useful in determining the stage of fibrosis and the grade of inflammation for individuals affected by chronic hepatitis B (HBV) and/or hepatitis C (HCV). However, there are increasing biochemical and virological measurements that are aiding in prognostication for these diseases. [10] In patients with chronic HCV infection, comorbidities such as chronic alcohol use and/or hepatic steatosis have correlated with more rapid liver fibrosis progression. [11] Also, in patients with hemochromatosis, those evaluated with biopsy revealing advanced fibrosis were at increased risk of developing hepatocellular cancer (HCC) compared to those without fibrosis. [12]

retrieved_docs.title: Viral Hepatidities (Archived) -- Histopathology
retrieved_docs.content: A biopsy is usually not done for acute viral hepatitis. Biopsies are done if there clinical suspicion of a second independent hepatic insult such as underlying chronic liver disease or if there are unusual infectious processes in immunocompromised patients. All forms of acute hepatitis show lobular disarray. This includes ballooning degeneration, spotty necrosis, Kupffer cell hyperplasia, canalicular cholestasis, hepatocellular regeneration, sinusoidal and lobular mononuclear cell infiltrate, and scattered apoptotic bodies.

retrieved_docs.title: Hepatitis A -- Histopathology
retrieved_docs.content: Liver biopsy is rarely performed in uncomplicated acute hepatitis A infections. When a biopsy is performed, histopathological examination reveals inflammation and necrosis in the periportal regions with varied levels of apoptosis. [20] [21] While cholestasis accompanied by an increased presence of plasma cells in the portal and periportal areas can be more indicative of hepatitis A, definitive diagnosis necessitates laboratory confirmation. In cases of severe hepatitis, necrotic bridges may form between portal areas.

retrieved_docs.title: Viral Hepatidities (Archived) -- Consultations
retrieved_docs.content: Hepatology, gastroenterology, and infectious disease specialists are usually consulted.

retrieved_docs.title: Hepatitis C -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: A liver biopsy is not routine, but it may help determine the severity of the disease. Other indications for a liver biopsy include An uncertain diagnosis The presence of another liver disorder An immunocompromised patient

retrieved_docs.title: Viral Hepatitis -- Evaluation -- Hepatitis C
retrieved_docs.content: Assessment of hepatic fibrosis is an important step in evaluating those confirmed to have HCV infection. The degree of fibrosis plays a role in determining the treatment regimen and duration if there is advanced (bridging) fibrosis or cirrhosis. Fibrosis stage can be assessed by indirect markers readily found in routine blood tests such as the AST/ALT ratio, the AST to platelet ratio index (APRI), and the Fibrosis-4 score (online calculators available). Noninvasive assessment of fibrosis can also be obtained via ultrasound-based transient elastography. Liver biopsy has previously been accepted as the gold standard of fibrosis assessment. However, it is an invasive procedure with a small risk of complications not limited to bleeding and infection. In addition, the high cost and sampling error makes it a less desirable option. [36] [37] [38] Liver biopsy is generally reserved for cases of concern for a competing or concomitant diagnosis. (see Image . Liver Biopsy Hematoxylin and Eosin Stain).

retrieved_docs.title: Viral Hepatitis -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: The pathophysiology of viral hepatitis varies with the type of hepatitis pathogen.

retrieved_docs.title: Viral Hepatitis -- Prognosis
retrieved_docs.content: The prognosis of viral hepatitis depends on the type of virus causing the infection.

retrieved_docs.title: Autoimmune Hepatitis -- Histopathology
retrieved_docs.content: The hallmark histologic feature of autoimmune hepatitis is interface hepatitis. However, it is nondiagnostic as it is present in most cases of viral hepatitis. Various histological findings are associated with autoimmune hepatitis, but all of them are nonspecific.

retrieved_docs.title: Viral Hepatidities (Archived) -- Pearls and Other Issues
retrieved_docs.content: New guidelines endorsed by the United States Preventive Services Task Force indicate screening for Hepatitic C virus infection in all adults aged 18 to 79 even without a history of significant exposure or risk factors.

retrieved_docs.title: Hepatitis C -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: Further evaluation consists of checking the viral genotype, which is still important in choosing the most optimal regimen and predicting the response to therapy. Other baseline evaluations include testing for HIV, hepatitis B surface antigen, susceptibility to hepatitis A and hepatitis B virus infections, and screening for other underlying causes of liver disease such as autoimmune liver disease, hemochromatosis, and Wilson disease. Before determining the HCV treatment strategy, the next step is to stage the disease, utilizing liver biopsy (gold standard) or approved imaging modalities with or without noninvasive biomarkers. Lastly, these patients should also undergo variceal screening and screening for hepatocellular carcinoma. [9] [10] [11] Other studies include: INR, prothrombin time Complete blood count Liver function tests GFR Serology for other hepatic viruses Thyroid function Screening for drug abuse and alcohol Mental health assessment

retrieved_docs.title: Viral Hepatitis -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: In addition to these initial routine laboratory tests, other specific tests are available to evaluate for the type of viral hepatitis as follows:

retrieved_docs.title: Viral Hepatidities (Archived) -- Treatment Planning
retrieved_docs.content: Monitoring and follow-ups during the treatment period are extensive, and therefore, treatment planning should involve a gastroenterologist or a hepatologist. Furthermore, although there are now treatments available that are pan-genotypic, genotype testing is usually still performed to tailor and individualized treatment plans.

retrieved_docs.title: Viral Hepatidities (Archived) -- Review Questions
retrieved_docs.content: Access free multiple choice questions on this topic. Comment on this article.

retrieved_docs.title: Hepatitis B -- Histopathology
retrieved_docs.content: Acute Hepatitis B Infection: Histologic findings include "lobular disarray, ballooning degeneration, multiple apoptotic bodies, Kupffer cell activation, and lymphocyte-predominant lobular and portal inflammation. [9] Chronic Hepatitis B Infection: Lymphocyte-predominant portal inflammation with interface hepatitis and spotty lobular inflammation. [9]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 3 -- Community 1856
retrieved_docs.content: الاعتماد على الكحول (alcohol dependence) is clinically associated with اِلْتِهَابُ الكَبِد (liver inflammation), indicating that chronic alcohol use can contribute to inflammatory damage of the liver. Hepatitis is classified as اِلْتِهَابُ الكَبِد, meaning that hepatitis represents a specific type of liver inflammation often caused by viral infection, autoimmune processes, or toxic insults. Together, these relationships highlight that liver inflammation can arise both from alcohol dependence and from hepatitis, underscoring the importance of evaluating alcohol use and identifying hepatitis when assessing patients with hepatic inflammatory conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 3 -- Community 2620
retrieved_docs.content: Alcoholic hepatitis (تَشَمَّعٌ كُحولِيّ) is a liver disorder caused by excessive alcohol consumption that can progress to hepatic cirrhosis (تَشَمُّع كَبِدي). This relationship highlights the potential evolution from an inflammatory, potentially reversible condition to an irreversible stage of extensive liver scarring, emphasizing the importance of early detection and treatment of alcoholic hepatitis to prevent the development of cirrhosis.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 12 -- Level 1 -- Community 210
retrieved_docs.content: modalities, procedural interventions, and disease processes are anatomically and clinically related, providing a comprehensive framework for the evaluation and management of abdominal, hepatic, and biliary disorders.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2676
retrieved_docs.content: Cancer of the liver and intrahepatic bile duct manifests as سرطان الخلايا الكبدية (liver cell carcinoma), which is broadly related to مَرَض كَبِدِيّ صَفْراوِيّ (jaundiced liver disease). This connection indicates that hepatocellular carcinoma often presents within the spectrum of liver diseases that cause jaundice, highlighting the importance of recognizing jaundice as a potential clinical clue to underlying malignant liver pathology. Understanding this relationship aids clinicians in early detection, accurate diagnosis, and appropriate management of patients with liver‑related malignancies and associated jaundice.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2675
retrieved_docs.content: Hepatitis is the clinical term that classifies the condition known in Arabic as اِلْتِهَابُ الكَبِد, which refers to inflammation of the liver.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 18 -- Level 2 -- Community 655
retrieved_docs.content: Antivirals for treatment of HCV infections include ribavirin and telaprevir, both of which are employed as therapeutic agents against hepatitis C virus. Remdesivir has been identified as a medication that may treat عدوى فيروسية (viral infection), while ribavirin is also noted for its potential to prevent عدوى فيروسية. Together, these antiviral drugs illustrate the spectrum of strategies used to both treat and prevent viral diseases, underscoring their importance in clinical management of infections such as hepatitis C and other viral pathogens.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 14 -- Level 2 -- Community 1151
retrieved_docs.content: Hepatitis (اِلْتِهَابُ الكَبِد) is classified as liver inflammation. It is clinically associated with both alcohol‑abstinence syndrome (متلازمة الامتناع عن تعاطي الكحول) and hepatocellular carcinoma (سرطان الخلايا الكبدية). Hepatocellular carcinoma is a manifestation of cancer of the liver and intrahepatic bile duct and also has a broader relationship with cholestatic liver disease (مَرَض كَبِدِيّ صَفْراوِيّ). These interconnections highlight the role of hepatitis in the development of liver cancer and related hepatic disorders, emphasizing the importance of monitoring and managing hepatitis to prevent progression to malignancy and other liver complications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 2 -- Community 262
retrieved_docs.content: The therapeutic category “Antivirals for treatment of HCV infections” comprises agents such as ribavirin and telaprevir, both of which are employed as antiviral medications specifically to manage hepatitis C virus (HCV) infection.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 5 -- Level 3 -- Community 2074
retrieved_docs.content: Hepatitis, which is classified as اِلْتِهَابُ الكَبِد (liver inflammation), is clinically associated with متلازمة الامتناع عن تعاطي الكحول (alcohol withdrawal syndrome). This relationship indicates that patients experiencing alcohol abstinence may also develop or have concurrent liver inflammation, underscoring the importance of monitoring hepatic function and providing appropriate liver‑focused care during the management of alcohol withdrawal.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 3 -- Community 2274
retrieved_docs.content: Alcoholic cirrhosis (تَشَمَّعٌ كُحولِيّ) is a specific subtype of liver cirrhosis (تَشَمُّع كَبِدي). Liver cirrhosis, in turn, is clinically associated with esophageal variceal bleeding (نزيف دَوالِي المريء). Recognizing that patients with alcoholic cirrhosis develop the broader condition of liver cirrhosis helps clinicians anticipate the high risk of variceal hemorrhage, prompting appropriate screening, monitoring, and preventive interventions to improve patient outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 2 -- Community 369
retrieved_docs.content: Ribavirin is an antiviral medication that belongs to the class of antivirals used for the treatment of hepatitis C virus (HCV) infections, making it a member of the therapeutic group “Antivirals for treatment of HCV infections.”

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 2 -- Community 1206
retrieved_docs.content: Cancer of liver and intrahepatic bile duct is a malignant condition that manifests as سرطان الخلايا الكبدية (liver cell cancer). Hepatitis (اِلْتِهَابُ الكَبِد), which is classified as liver inflammation, is clinically associated with سرطان الخلايا الكبدية, indicating that patients with hepatitis may have an increased risk of developing this type of liver cancer. Moreover, سرطان الخلايا الكبدية has a broader relationship with مَرَض كَبِدِيّ صَفْراوِيّ, a jaundiced liver disease, suggesting that liver cell cancer can be part of or contribute to the clinical spectrum of jaundice‑related hepatic disorders. Recognizing the interconnections among cancer of liver and intrahepatic bile duct, hepatitis, and مَرَض كَبِدِيّ صَفْراوِيّ is essential for comprehensive diagnosis and management of hepatic pathology.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 2 -- Community 285
retrieved_docs.content: Ribavirin is classified as a member of the group of antivirals used for the treatment of hepatitis C virus (HCV) infections, indicating its role within this therapeutic class. In addition to its use against HCV, ribavirin may also help prevent viral infections (عدوى فيروسية). Remdesivir, another antiviral agent, may be employed to treat viral infections (عدوى فيروسية). Together, ribavirin and remdesivir exemplify antiviral medications that are utilized both for treating specific viral diseases such as HCV and for broader management of viral infections, underscoring their importance in antiviral therapy and patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 2 -- Community 638
retrieved_docs.content: Antivirals for treatment of HCV infections are classified as direct‑acting antivirals, and telaprevir is a member of this antiviral group used specifically to treat hepatitis C virus infection.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 24 -- Level 1 -- Community 175
retrieved_docs.content: diseases and can be precipitated by substances that are also used in invasive interventions, including abdominal paracentesis (الاسْتِسْقَاء), incision and excision of the central nervous system, injections and aspirations of muscles, tendons, bursae, joints and soft tissue, non‑therapeutic or male genital procedures, and therapeutic endocrine procedures. Hepatitis (اِلْتِهَابُ الكَبِد) represents liver inflammation and is clinically associated with hepatocellular carcinoma (سرطان الخلايا الكبدية), a cancer of the liver and intra‑hepatic bile ducts that is further related to jaundiced liver disease (مَرَض كَبِدِيّ صَفْراوِيّ). Primary liver cancers and intra‑hepatic bile‑duct cancers are also located in the liver. Liver biopsy (خزعة الكبد) is a partially excised‑anatomy procedure that obtains tissue for histologic evaluation. Liver ultrasound (فحص الكبد بالموجات فوق الصوتية) is a child procedure of gastrointestinal‑tract ultrasound and of diagnostic ultrasound of the gastrointestinal

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 3 -- Community 2341
retrieved_docs.content: Failure (biologic function) is associated with الفَشَلٌ الكَبِدِيُّ الكُلْوِيّ, indicating that a general loss of biological function can be linked to hepatorenal failure—a severe condition in which both liver and kidney functions are compromised. Recognizing this association is important for clinicians, as it highlights the need to assess and manage systemic organ failure comprehensively in affected patients.
